sentence_reviews.text: The learner chooses informative data or follows an ordered progression from easier to harder experience.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Datasets, sampling & splits.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Active learning & curriculum learning follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19 'Learning from Examples', 19.2 'Supervised Learning', and 19.6.5 'Active learning'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Active learning appears under learning from examples; curriculum learning is not directly named, so the combined-node rationale is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Active learning & curriculum learning is modeled as depending on Supervised learning, Datasets, sampling & splits and feeding downstream nodes such as Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules, AutoML & automated model design.
